summary:
The lower lip typically sits close to or up to 2 mm behind the pogonion as per female-specific criteria outlined by Silver’s methodological approach used in defining ideal facial aesthetics based upon Ricketts’ planes.

Agent1's reasoning (Round 2): Considering the provided information about Pogonionic position relative to an imaginary horizontal line through nasolabial groove apex, which serves as one boundary marker; along another vertical midline from external auditory meatus down towards mentum serving secondarily – when combined they form basis for determining optimal placement regarding lips especially lower ones where slight variations can significantly impact overall appearance due sensitivity around these areas making precise measurements crucial during assessments like those conducted under Rickets system focusing heavily onto symmetry & proportionality aspects inherent across different anatomical features including mouth region particularly important here given its central role both functionally aesthetically speaking thus ensuring proper alignment between them becomes paramount aspect influencing final outcome quality wise irrespective whether male/female patient undergoing evaluation process currently underway at hand right now.
